exec: console.warn('[Postman] Login did not return an accessToken. Check credentials.');

item.name: Login (User)
item.description:
User login with optional guest cart migration.

OPTIONAL: Include guestSessionId to migrate guest cart items to authenticated user account.

If guest added items to cart before logging in:
1. Guest items will be automatically merged into user's cart during login
2. If same product exists in both carts, quantities are combined
3. Guest session is cleared after migration

exec: console.warn('[Postman] Login (User) did not return an accessToken.');

item.name: List Products (Public)
item.description:
List all products with extensive filtering and sorting options.

📋 FILTER PARAMETERS:

1. **Category Filtering:**
   - categoryId (string): Filter products by category UUID

2. **Series & Model Filtering:**
   - seriesId (string): Filter by device series UUID (e.g., Samsung Galaxy S series)
   - deviceModelId (string): Filter by specific device model UUID (e.g., Galaxy S26 Ultra)

3. **Condition Filtering:**
   - conditionId (string): Filter by product condition UUID (e.g., NEW, EXCELLENT, GOOD)

4. **Option Filtering (Multiple values create AND logic):**
   - colorId (string): Filter products with specific color UUID
   - storageOptionId (string): Filter products with storage option UUID (e.g., 256GB)
   - ramOptionId (string): Filter products with RAM option UUID (e.g., 12GB)
   - Example: colorId=red&storageOptionId=256gb returns products with BOTH red color AND 256GB storage

5. **Price Range:**
   - priceMin (number): Minimum price filter (e.g., 500)
   - priceMax (number): Maximum price filter (e.g., 1500)
   - Filters products where basePrice is between priceMin and priceMax (inclusive)

6. **Text Search:**
   - search (string): Full-text search across product title and description (case-insensitive)
   - Partial matches work (e.g., search=galaxy returns Galaxy S26, Galaxy Z, etc.)

7. **Product Status:**
   - listingStatus (string): Filter by listing status - ACTIVE or INACTIVE
   - isFeatured (string/boolean): true or false - filters featured products

8. **Sorting:**
   - sortBy (string): Sort order
     • "priceAsc" - Sort by price (lowest to highest)
     • "priceDesc" - Sort by price (highest to lowest)     
     • "featured" - Sort by featured status (featured first)
     • Default (if not provided) - Sorted by newest created

9. **Pagination:**
   - page (number): Page number (default: 1, minimum: 1)
   - limit (number): Items per page (default: 24, maximum: 100)
   - Skip = (page - 1) × limit

⚙️ FILTER LOGIC:
- All filters are OPTIONAL and can be combined
- Multiple filters use AND logic (all conditions must match)
- Multiple values for same filter type (color, storage, RAM) use AND logic
- Returns: { success, data: [products], meta: {total, page, limit, ...} }

🎯 EXAMPLES:
1. GET /api/public/product?categoryId=abc&priceMin=100&priceMax=500&page=1&limit=20
   → Returns products in category 'abc' between $100-$500, first 20 results

2. GET /api/public/product?seriesId=xyz&colorId=red&storageOptionId=256&sortBy=priceAsc
   → Returns Galaxy S series products with red color and 256GB storage, sorted by price low-to-high

3. GET /api/public/product?search=iphone&listingStatus=ACTIVE&isFeatured=true
   → Returns active featured products matching 'iphone'

4. GET /api/public/product?deviceModelId=123&ramOptionId=12gb&limit=50
   → Returns specific device model with 12GB RAM option, 50 items per page

query.description: Filter products by category UUID. Returns only products in the specified category.
Filter by device series UUID (e.g., Samsung Galaxy S series). Returns products in that series.
Filter by specific device model UUID (e.g., Galaxy S26 Ultra). Returns products of that exact model.
Filter by product condition UUID (NEW, EXCELLENT, GOOD, FAIR). Returns products matching the condition.
Filter products by color UUID. When combined with other filters, uses AND logic (must match ALL conditions).
Filter products by storage option UUID (256GB, 512GB, 1TB, etc.). AND logic with other filters.
Filter products by RAM option UUID (8GB, 12GB, 16GB, etc.). AND logic with other filters.
Minimum price threshold (number). Returns products where basePrice >= priceMin.
Maximum price threshold (number). Returns products where basePrice <= priceMax. Use with priceMin for range filtering.
Full-text search (case-insensitive). Searches in product title and description. Partial matches work (e.g., 'galaxy' returns Galaxy S26, Galaxy Z, etc.)
Page number for pagination (default: 1, minimum: 1). Skip calculation: (page - 1) × limit
Items per page (default: 24, maximum: 100). Controls how many products are returned per page.
Sort order: 'priceAsc' (low to high), 'priceDesc' (high to low), 'featured' (featured first), or omit for newest first (default).

item.name: Update Profile (Auth)
item.description:
Update authenticated user's profile information.

Personal Information Fields:
- firstName (string): User's first name
- lastName (string): User's last name  
- phone (string): User's phone number

Address Fields (in addresses array):
- fullName (string, optional): Full name for address. Auto-generated from firstName + lastName if not provided
- phone (string, optional): Phone number for address
- street (string, required): Street address
- city (string, required): City
- state (string, optional): State/Province
- zipCode (string, required): Postal code
- country (string, required): Country
- isDefault (boolean, optional): Set as default address

Rules:
- At least one field must be provided
- Email, role, and status cannot be updated
- You can update personal info, addresses, or both
- If no fullName provided for address, auto-generated from updated firstName + lastName
- Response includes all updated data with addresses

item.name: Validate Promo Code (Auth)
item.description:
Validate a promo code against the authenticated user's cart.

📋 FIELDS:
- promoCode (string, required): The promo code to validate
- cartItemIds (array, optional): Specific cart item IDs to validate against. If omitted, validates against ALL cart items.

💡 USE CASE: If the user has 5 items in cart but is only checking out 3, send those 3 cart item IDs. The discount and finalTotal will be calculated for those 3 items only. Pass the same cartItemIds to the checkout request.

item.name: Get My Orders (Auth)
item.description:
Retrieve authenticated user's orders with pagination and optional status filtering.

📋 FILTER PARAMETERS:

1. **Status Filtering (optional):**
   - status (string): Filter orders by order status
     • PENDING - Order created, awaiting payment
     • PROCESSING - Payment received, order being prepared
     • SHIPPED - Order sent to customer
     • DELIVERED - Order delivered to customer
     • CANCELLED - Order cancelled
   - If not provided: Returns ALL user's orders regardless of status

2. **Pagination:**
   - page (number): Page number (default: 1, minimum: 1)
   - limit (number): Items per page (default: 50, maximum: 100)
   - Skip = (page - 1) × limit

🔒 Auth required - User sees only their own orders

💾 RESPONSE FIELDS:
- id: Order UUID
- stringId: Order display ID (e.g., ORD-ABC123)
- orderStatus: Status (PENDING, PROCESSING, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED)
- totalPrice: Final price after discounts
- shippingCost: Shipping charges
- discountTotal: Applied discount amount
- promoCodeUsed: Promo code if applied
- paymentStatus: PENDING, AUTHORIZED, PAID, FAILED
- paymentMethod: STRIPE or other payment method
- shippingAddress: Full address object
- orderItems: Array of items with product details
- createdAt, updatedAt: Timestamps

🎯 EXAMPLES:
1. GET /api/orders?page=1&limit=10
   → Returns user's first 10 orders (all statuses)

2. GET /api/orders?status=DELIVERED&page=1&limit=20
   → Returns user's delivered orders

3. GET /api/orders?status=PENDING
   → Returns user's pending orders

4. GET /api/orders?status=CANCELLED&page=2&limit=15
   → Returns user's cancelled orders (page 2)

📊 PAGINATION METADATA:
- Response includes meta with: total, page, limit, totalPages, count, hasNext, hasPrev

query.description: Page number for pagination (default: 1). Skip = (page - 1) × limit
Items per page (default: 50, maximum: 100). Controls how many orders are returned per page.
Filter by order status: PENDING, PROCESSING, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, or CANCELLED. If omitted, returns all orders.

item.name: Shop Now - Direct Product Checkout (Auth)
item.description: Direct product purchase without adding to cart. User can buy a product directly from product detail page.

item.name: Add To Cart (Guest)
item.description:
Add product to cart as guest user without authentication.
Guest must provide guestSessionId (UUID) to track cart across sessions.

📋 REQUIRED PARAMETERS:
- guestSessionId (string): Unique session identifier (e.g., UUID)
- productId (string): Product UUID
- colorId (string): Selected color UUID
- storageOptionId (string): Selected storage option UUID
- ramOptionId (string): Selected RAM option UUID
- quantity (number): Quantity to add (default: 1)

🎯 FLOW:
1. Frontend generates guestSessionId (UUID) and stores in localStorage
2. Send guestSessionId with each request
3. Cart automatically created for new session
4. Duplicate configs increase quantity instead of creating duplicate items

item.name: Get Cart (Guest)
item.description:
Retrieve guest cart with all items and totals.

📋 REQUIRED PARAMETERS:
- guestSessionId (string, query param): Guest session identifier

💾 RESPONSE:
- items: Array of cart items with product details
- subtotal: Sum of all item totals
- totalItems: Total quantity of items in cart

item.name: Update Cart Item (Guest)
item.description: Update quantity of item in guest cart.

item.name: Remove Cart Item (Guest)
item.description: Remove specific item from guest cart.

item.name: Clear Cart (Guest)
item.description: Remove all items from guest cart.

item.name: Validate Promo Code (Guest)
item.description:
Validate a promo code against the guest cart before checkout. No authentication required — pass guestSessionId in the body.

📋 FIELDS:
- guestSessionId (string, required): Guest session identifier
- promoCode (string, required): The promo code to validate
- cartItemIds (array, optional): Specific cart item IDs to validate against. If omitted, validates against ALL guest cart items.

💡 USE CASE: If the guest has 5 items in cart but is only checking out 3, send those 3 cart item IDs. The discount and finalTotal will be calculated for those 3 items only. Pass the same cartItemIds to the checkout request.

item.name: Create Checkout Session (Guest)
item.description:
Create checkout session for guest user from cart.

📋 REQUIRED PARAMETERS:
- guestSessionId (string): Guest session identifier
- guestEmail (string): Guest email for order tracking
- shippingAddress (object): Delivery address with fields: fullName, street, city, zipCode, country
- cartItemIds (array, optional): Specific items to checkout. If empty, all items checked out
- shippingMethod (string, optional): Delivery method (e.g., "Standard Delivery")
- shippingCost (number, optional): Shipping charges (default: 0)
- promoCode (string, optional): Discount code

✅ FLOW:
1. Guest provides email (required for order tracking)
2. Cart items fetched and validated
3. Order created with guestEmail and guest session
4. Stripe checkout session generated
5. Returns checkout URL for payment

item.name: Confirm Checkout Session (Guest)
item.description:
Confirm payment for guest checkout after Stripe payment completion.

📋 PARAMETERS:
- sessionId (string): Stripe checkout session ID (returned from checkout creation)

✅ RESPONSE:
- Returns confirmed order details
- Updates order status to PROCESSING
- Updates payment status to PAID

item.name: Create Series
item.description:
Create a new series with optional image upload.

📋 FIELDS:
- name (string, required): Series name (e.g., "iPhone", "Samsung Galaxy")
- image (file, optional): Series image file (images only, max 5MB)

📝 HOW TO USE:
1. Open this request in Postman
2. Go to Body tab
3. Select "form-data"
4. Add text field: key="name", value="iPhone"
5. Add file field: key="image", select your image file
6. Send the request

item.name: Update Series
item.description:
Update an existing series with optional image upload.

📋 FIELDS:
- name (string, optional): Series name to update
- image (file, optional): New series image file (images only, max 5MB)

📝 HOW TO USE:
1. Open this request in Postman
2. Go to Body tab
3. Select "form-data"
4. Add fields you want to update:
   - Text field: key="name", value="Samsung Galaxy"
   - File field: key="image", select your image file
5. You can update one or both fields
6. Send the request

📌 NOTE: Both fields are optional. If not provided, only that data is updated.

item.name: Get Condition Model Price By ID
Dashboard Overview (Admin)
item.description:
Admin dashboard endpoint to retrieve overview statistics and KPIs.

🔒 Admin-only endpoint - requires adminToken

📊 RESPONSE:
Returns aggregated statistics including:
- Total orders and revenue
- Active users
- Recent orders summary
- Top-selling products
- Order status breakdown (PENDING, PROCESSING, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED)
- Dashboard widgets data

📝 NOTE:
- No query parameters available for this endpoint
- Returns current/global statistics
- Use /api/admin/dashboard/revenue-overview?year=XXXX for year-specific revenue filtering

item.name: Revenue Overview (Admin)
item.description:
Admin dashboard endpoint to retrieve revenue statistics filtered by year.

📋 FILTER PARAMETERS:

1. **Year Filtering (optional):**
   - year (number): Filter revenue by specific year (e.g., 2026)
   - If not provided: Returns data for current year
   - Default: Current calendar year

⚙️ RESPONSE:
- Returns monthly revenue breakdown for the specified year
- Includes:
  • Monthly revenue totals
  • Order count per month
  • Average order value
  • Total revenue for the year
  • Growth statistics

🔒 Admin-only endpoint - requires adminToken

🎯 EXAMPLES:
1. GET /api/admin/dashboard/revenue-overview?year=2026
   → Returns complete revenue data for 2026 (January - December)

2. GET /api/admin/dashboard/revenue-overview?year=2025
   → Returns revenue data for 2025

3. GET /api/admin/dashboard/revenue-overview
   → Returns revenue data for current year (if not specified)

📊 TYPICAL RESPONSE FORMAT:
- months: Array with monthly data
  • month: Month number (1-12)
  • monthName: Month name
  • revenue: Total revenue for month
  • orders: Order count for month
  • avgOrderValue: Average order value
- summary: Yearly summary
  • totalRevenue: Total revenue for year
  • totalOrders: Total orders for year
  • avgOrderValue: Average order value for year

query.description: Filter revenue by year (e.g., 2026). Returns monthly revenue breakdown for that year.

2. **Series & Model Filtering:**

   - deviceModelId (string): Filter by specific device model UUID

3. **Condition Filtering:**

   - storageOptionId (string): Filter products with storage option
   - ramOptionId (string): Filter products with RAM option

9. **Pagination:**

⚙️ RESPONSE:

item.name: Get Contacts (Admin)
item.description:
Admin endpoint to list all contact messages with pagination and sorting.

📋 FILTER PARAMETERS:

1. **Pagination:**
   - page (number): Page number (default: 1, minimum: 1)
   - limit (number): Items per page (default: 10, maximum: 100)
   - Skip = (page - 1) × limit

⚙️ SORTING:
- Results sorted by createdAt in descending order (newest first)
- No additional sorting options

🔒 Admin-only endpoint - requires adminToken

💾 RESPONSE FIELDS:
- id: Contact message UUID
- firstName: Sender's first name
- lastName: Sender's last name
- email: Sender's email address
- phone: Sender's phone number
- subject: Message subject
- message: Message content
- status: Contact status (NEW, PENDING, CONTRACTED)
- createdAt: Submission timestamp

🎯 EXAMPLES:
1. GET /api/admin/contacts?page=1&limit=10
   → Returns first 10 contact messages

2. GET /api/admin/contacts?page=2&limit=20
   → Returns contact messages on page 2 (20 per page)

3. GET /api/admin/contacts?limit=50
   → Returns first 50 contact messages

📊 PAGINATION METADATA:
- Response includes meta with: total, page, limit, totalPages, count, hasNext, hasPrev
- hasStatus indicates whether status field is available in response

item.name: Get PromoCodes (Admin)
item.description:
Admin endpoint to list all promo/discount codes with pagination, search, and status filtering.

📋 FILTER PARAMETERS:

1. **Status Filter (optional):**
   - status (string): Filter by promo status
     • active   — isActive=true AND not yet expired
     • disabled — isActive=false AND not yet expired
     • expired  — expiryDate has passed (regardless of isActive)
   - If omitted: Returns all promo codes

2. **Text Search (optional):**
   - q (string): Search promo codes by code name (case-insensitive partial match)

3. **Pagination:**
   - page (number): Page number (default: 1, minimum: 1)
   - limit (number): Items per page (default: 20, maximum: 100)

🎯 EXAMPLES:
1. GET /api/admin/promocodes?status=active
   → Returns active, non-expired promo codes

2. GET /api/admin/promocodes?status=disabled
   → Returns disabled (isActive=false) non-expired codes

3. GET /api/admin/promocodes?status=expired
   → Returns all expired promo codes

4. GET /api/admin/promocodes?q=SUMMER&status=active
   → Active codes containing "SUMMER"

query.description: Page number for pagination (default: 1).
Items per page (default: 20, maximum: 100).
Filter by status: active | expired
Search by promo code name (case-insensitive partial match).

item.name: Get All Users
item.description:
Admin endpoint to list all customer users with pagination and search.

📋 FILTER PARAMETERS:

1. **Text Search (optional):**
   - q (string): Search across multiple fields using AND logic:
     • firstName (case-insensitive partial match)
     • lastName (case-insensitive partial match)
     • email (case-insensitive partial match)
     • phone (case-insensitive partial match)
   - Example: q=john returns users with "john" in firstName, lastName, email, or phone

2. **Role Filter (optional):**
   - role (string): Filter by user role
     • Default: CUSTOMER (if not specified)
     • Values: CUSTOMER, ADMIN
   - Note: This endpoint always returns CUSTOMER users by default

3. **Pagination:**
   - page (number): Page number (default: 1, minimum: 1)
   - limit (number): Items per page (default: 50, maximum: 100)
   - Skip = (page - 1) × limit

🔒 Admin-only endpoint - requires adminToken

⚙️ RESPONSE:
- Returns paginated list of users with metadata
- meta includes: total, page, limit, totalPages, count, hasNext, hasPrev
- User fields: id, email, firstName, lastName, phone, role, status, isEmailVerified, createdAt

🎯 EXAMPLES:
1. GET /api/admin/users?page=1&limit=20
   → Returns first 20 customers

2. GET /api/admin/users?q=john&limit=10
   → Returns customers matching "john" (up to 10 results)

3. GET /api/admin/users?q=john@example.com&page=2&limit=50
   → Returns customers with email containing "john@example.com" (page 2, 50 per page)

query.description: Page number for pagination (default: 1). Skip = (page - 1) × limit
Items per page (default: 50, maximum: 100). Controls how many users are returned per page.
Search query - searches firstName, lastName, email, phone fields with AND logic (case-insensitive). Optional parameter.
Filter by user role: CUSTOMER or ADMIN. Default is CUSTOMER if not specified. Optional parameter.

item.name: Get Sell Requests (Admin)
item.description:
Admin endpoint to list all sell requests with pagination, filtering, and search.

📋 FILTER PARAMETERS:

1. **Status Filtering (optional):**
   - status (string): Filter sell requests by status
     • SUBMITTED - Initial request submitted
     • RECEIVED - Device received by admin
     • INSPECTION - Device under inspection
     • OFFER_MADE - Price offer made to user
     • PAID - Payment completed
     • REJECTED - Request rejected
   - If not provided: Returns all requests regardless of status

2. **Device Model Filtering (optional):**
   - deviceModelId (string): Filter by specific device model UUID
   - Example: deviceModelId=abc123 returns requests for iPhone 14 Pro

3. **Text Search (optional):**
   - search (string): Search by user details (case-insensitive partial match)
     • Searches in: firstName, lastName, email, phone
     • AND logic applied (must match search term)
   - Example: search=john returns requests from "John Doe", "johnny", "john@email.com"

4. **Pagination:**
   - page (number): Page number (default: 1, minimum: 1)
   - limit (number): Items per page (default: 20, maximum: 100)
   - Skip = (page - 1) × limit

⚙️ SORTING:
- Results sorted by createdAt in descending order (newest first)

🔒 Admin-only endpoint - requires adminToken

💾 RESPONSE FIELDS:
- id: Sell request UUID
- firstName, lastName: Customer name
- email, phone: Customer contact
- deviceModelName: Device model name
- conditionName: Device condition
- baseOfferPrice: Admin's offered price
- userOfferedPrice: User's proposed price
- status: Request status
- createdAt, updatedAt: Timestamps
- isDeleted: Soft-delete status
- images: Array of device images

🎯 EXAMPLES:
1. GET /api/admin/sell-requests?page=1&limit=20
   → Returns first 20 sell requests (all statuses)

2. GET /api/admin/sell-requests?status=INSPECTION&page=1&limit=15
   → Returns sell requests currently under inspection

3. GET /api/admin/sell-requests?deviceModelId=xyz&page=1
   → Returns iPhone 14 Pro sell requests

4. GET /api/admin/sell-requests?search=john&status=OFFER_MADE&limit=10
   → Returns sell requests from "john" with offer made (10 per page)

5. GET /api/admin/sell-requests?status=PAID&limit=50&page=2
   → Returns paid sell requests (page 2, 50 per page)

📊 PAGINATION METADATA:
- Response includes meta with: page, limit, total, hasNext, hasPrevious

query.description: Page number for pagination (default: 1). Skip = (page - 1) × limit
Items per page (default: 20, maximum: 100). Controls how many sell requests are returned per page.
Filter by request status: SUBMITTED, RECEIVED, INSPECTION, OFFER_MADE, PAID, or REJECTED. Optional parameter.
Filter by device model UUID. Returns requests for specific device model. Optional parameter.
Search by user details - searches firstName, lastName, email, phone (case-insensitive partial match). Optional parameter.

request.description:
Admin endpoint to retrieve all orders with pagination and optional status filtering.

📋 FILTER PARAMETERS:

1. **Status Filtering (optional):**
   - status (string): Filter orders by order status
     • PENDING - Order created, awaiting payment
     • PROCESSING - Payment received, order being prepared
     • SHIPPED - Order sent to customer
     • DELIVERED - Order delivered to customer
     • CANCELLED - Order cancelled by user (includes cancelledAt and cancellationReason)
   - If not provided: Returns ALL orders regardless of status

2. **Pagination:**
   - page (number): Page number (default: 1, minimum: 1)
   - limit (number): Items per page (default: 50, maximum: 100)
   - Skip = (page - 1) × limit

🔒 Admin-only endpoint - requires adminToken

💾 RESPONSE FIELDS:
- id: Order UUID
- stringId: Order display ID (e.g., ORD-XYZ123)
- userId: Customer user ID
- orderStatus: Status (PENDING, PROCESSING, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED)
- totalPrice: Final price after discounts
- shippingCost: Shipping charges
- discountTotal: Applied discount amount
- promoCodeUsed: Promo code if applied
- paymentStatus: PENDING, AUTHORIZED, PAID, FAILED
- paymentMethod: STRIPE or other payment method
- shippingAddress: Full address object (fullName, phone, street, city, state, zipCode, country)
- orderItems: Array of items with product, color, storage, RAM details
- **cancelledAt**: Timestamp when order was cancelled (only for CANCELLED orders)
- **cancellationReason**: Reason provided by user for cancellation (only for CANCELLED orders)
- createdAt: Order creation timestamp
- updatedAt: Last update timestamp

🎯 EXAMPLES:
1. GET /api/admin/orders?page=1&limit=20
   → Returns all orders (all statuses), paginated

2. GET /api/admin/orders?status=CANCELLED&page=1&limit=50
   → Returns only cancelled orders with cancellation reasons

3. GET /api/admin/orders?status=PROCESSING&limit=10
   → Returns orders being processed (first 10)

4. GET /api/admin/orders?status=DELIVERED&page=2&limit=25
   → Returns delivered orders (page 2, 25 per page)

query.description: Page number for pagination (default: 1). Skip = (page - 1) × limit
Items per page (default: 50, maximum: 100). Controls how many orders are returned per page.
